title: Postmortem - Incident #5102: Third-Party Payment Gateway Latency
content:
Postmortem - Incident #5102: Third-Party Payment Gateway Latency

### Incident Summary
On April 15, customer transactions on the /charge endpoint started timing out with 504 status codes. Payment gateway responses took over 15 seconds, blocking thread pools.

### Root Cause
The external Stripe payment API endpoint was experiencing a global service disruption, leading to timeouts in our payment-service calls.

### Resolution
Since there was no internal fix for external Stripe latency, the team manually tripped the circuit breaker flag in the payment-service configuration. This immediately failed payment attempts locally with a 'Payment system down, please try again' message, freeing up thread pools and restoring general API stability.

### Action Items
- Configure automatic circuit breaking with a timeout of 3 seconds.
- Add secondary gateway fallback (e.g. Adyen, PayPal).
